description_long: This is a compact hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a natural grain pattern and a dark, weathered finish. The handle is ergonomically shaped, tapering towards the end for a secure grip, and appears to be made from a dense hardwood. The axe head is forged from dark metal, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade and a pronounced cutting edge, suitable for chopping or splitting tasks. The blade has a flat poll opposite the edge, allowing for hammering, and is securely mounted onto the handle using a traditional wedged fitting. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements, making it ideal for practical use in urban environments such as parks, garages, or maintenance areas. The axe shows signs of wear, suggesting it is a well-used and reliable tool.
description: A compact hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a dark, wedge-shaped metal blade, designed for practical chopping and splitting tasks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle and the broad, dark metal blade with a visible cutting edge.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the narrow profile of the blade and the oval cross-section of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's curvature and the flat poll of the axe head.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the end of the handle and the back of the axe head, showing the mounting area.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with an ergonomic, oval cross-section and a natural grain finish, designed for a secure grip.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad wedge-shaped blade with a straight cutting edge and a flat poll; no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged fitting securing the blade to the wooden handle.
